programmes.show_description: Lost in Translation: Another gruelling case for James Bolam and the experienced ex-police officers. The team investigate a crucified body found near Waltham Forest in 1996.
Pilot: Miep Gies agrees to help Otto Frank hide in the secret annexe of his business premises in Amsterdam, along with members of the van Pels and Pfeffer families.
Tinker, Tailor: Comedy from the famed Essex siblings. Sharon begins to wonder if her husband was right when he told her there was a 'grass' planted in his cell.
Sparring with Death: First Chapter: When Mattie investigates a murder at Wildemarsh Wellness Retreat, she finds several people with unhealthy intentions towards each other.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
When the Snow Lay Round About: It's two nights before Christmas, and the whole of Sun Hill is desperately hoping there won't be a shout. Pretty unlikely!
Blowing It All Away: PC Rickman becomes increasingly friendly with a drug user and soon comes under suspicion of using drugs herself.
All Change: Sun Hill's CID join the uniform beat on the biggest night of the century - the eve of the new millennium.
In the Firing Line: Smith goes undercover as a contract killer in a drug-dealing feud, but his target is a little too close for comfort.
Thug on the Tyne: Part 1: Frank Burnside goes away from the manor as he asks for Rawton's help in a major undercover operation in Newcastle.
A new midwife joins the team as the area is rocked by power cuts and blizzards, but she falls ill as soon as she arrives and soon has to assist with a difficult birth.
Lucille is on duty when a friend of Valerie's goes into labour - but when the patient's health deteriorates, the new recruit is accused of incompetence by her racist mother.
Atmospheric, gripping feature-length drama set in Victorian Newcastle, adapted from the best-seller. Orphaned Millie is taken in by a local rag woman, and a new life begins.
My Brother's Keeper: Sister Reginald's ex-con brother Alfie shows up at the convent. The sisters agree to let him stay, but when an artist is found murdered, all eyes are on Alfie.
Look to the Lady - Part One: Fifth 'Doctor Who' Peter Davison's next role was the mysterious 1930s detective in this gripping series. Campion battles to save a relic from art thieves.
Heads, I Win. Tails, You Lose.: Harry and Fergus take on a disturbing case - a serial killer targeting random victims and leaving a coin in their mouths. Heads, I Win. Tails, You Lose.
In Plain Sight: Emilia Fox stars in a feature-length forensics mystery. The accidental fatal shooting of a Polish teenager puts a firearms division under immense public scrutiny.
Final Curtain: A gritty murder mystery for those long in the tooth cops. The team return to the 1992 case of an actor, shot dead by his wife on stage. With guest Claire Bloom.
A new midwife joins the team as the area is rocked by power cuts and blizzards, but she falls ill as soon as she arrives and soon has to assist with a difficult birth.
Welcome to Switzerland: With the Frank family safe and secure, Miep's Jewish dentist asks for help hiding - can she find new ways to evade the Nazis?
Ice Cold: A multimillionaire philanthropist plans to donate his collection of Titanic memorabilia to the Ulster Maritime Museum, but is discovered dead before the press launch.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Nine and a Half Days: Another episode of the Essex sibling sitcom. Tracey organises a meeting of Prisoners' Wives and Sharon gets more involved than she expected.
Thug on the Tyne: Part 2: After Rawton's cover seems to have been blown, Burnside finds himself in deep water.
New. A Woman's Touch: Eva is called to investigate when a man is found dead in a car, and her suspicions soon turn to the deceased's wife.
New. Spice Wars: Michelle turns food critic to help a warring father and daughter to decide how to run their restaurant. Melody can't resist flirting with an old flame.
One More Chance: Connie puts Will under observation after a string of complaints, and a tense day ends in disaster when he accuses a seriously ill patient of faking her symptoms.
Altered States: Ruth's fragmented memories of a drunken night point to the possibility she was raped, and she's horrified when the man she thinks attacked her is admitted to the ED.
Thug on the Tyne: Part 2: After Rawton's cover seems to have been blown, Burnside finds himself in deep water.
New. As Masood wrestles with his conscience, Amira has an announcement. Shirley and Phil take matters in to their own hands to deal with Tasha's dad, and Jack pushes Ronnie away.
New. Masood stuns Christian with the family news while Syed struggles with his predicament. Ronnie declares her love to Jack, and Chelsea and Amira receive rather unexpected news.
The Call of the Wild: One of the best-loved series in the world, taking us back to the gentle world of 1930s Yorkshire. Calum has an article published in the Veterinary Record.
Ken Masters returns to Tarrant, determined to exact revenge on those who brought him down.
I No More Desire A Rose: Frank and Lu are hired to unmask a 'Voice in the Dark' who threatens to destroy Wintermas, and Sebastian faces his first solo case.
Someone Else's Baby: More laughs with the Essex sisters. Sharon and Tracey decide to search through their mother's belongings for undiscovered treasure.
The Man from Oswestry: Foggy Dewhurst proves to be a trouble-maker in the hit sitcom. It's the first episode for Brian Wilde, fresh from playing Mr Barraclough in 'Porridge'.
Mending Stuart's Leg: The late, great Bill Owen admires Nora's wrinkled stockings again in the vintage sitcom. The boys help a fellow old timer who has an odd medical problem.
The Great Boarding House Bathroom Caper: Classic sitcom with the three old boys growing old disgracefully. The trio are off to the seaside with Sid and Ivy - and Compo's ferrets.
Sour Grapes: Crime drama series set in the fictitious New Zealand town of Brokenwood. Shepherd investigates when a prominent wine critic is found dead inside a fermenting vat.
Final Curtain: A gritty murder mystery for those long in the tooth cops. The team return to the 1992 case of an actor, shot dead by his wife on stage. With guest Claire Bloom.
Chinese Walls: Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small are the rather easy-on-the-eye crimebusting duo in the hit series. Lawyer Emily Proctor is found axed to death in Hyde Park.
